title: Portfolio Scaffold
description: Build the HTML/CSS grid that will house this exact 130-week project tracker.
deliverable: A responsive, mobile-friendly webpage displaying a grid of project cards.
hints: Focus on CSS Grid for the layout.
Don't worry about JavaScript yet.
stretch_goals: Add a dark mode toggle button.

title: Interactive Task Manager
description: A step up from a basic to-do list. Add the ability to filter tasks and save them to the browser's Local Storage.
deliverable: A working task manager where data persists after page refresh.
hints: Use `localStorage.setItem()` to save the JavaScript array.
stretch_goals: Add a drag-and-drop feature to reorder tasks.

title: Zen Music Player UI
description: Build a clean, static interface for an audio player tailored for long meditation or focus tracks.
deliverable: A visual player with play/pause toggles, a progress bar, and volume controls.
hints: Use HTML5 `<audio>` tags for the actual media handling.
stretch_goals: Create a visual audio equalizer animation using CSS.

title: API Weather Dashboard
description: Build a dashboard that fetches real-time weather data from a free public API.
deliverable: A deployed web app displaying current temperature and dynamic weather icons.
hints: Use the native JavaScript `fetch()` API.
stretch_goals: Use Geolocation to fetch weather for the user's current location.

title: Custom Pomodoro Timer
description: Create a productivity timer with customizable work and break intervals.
deliverable: A timer that counts down, triggers an alarm sound, and logs completed sessions.
hints: Use `setInterval()` for the countdown logic.
stretch_goals: Add an SVG circular progress bar that empties as time ticks down.

title: Markdown Note Taker
description: A split-screen editor where you type Markdown on the left and see formatted HTML on the right.
deliverable: A functional Markdown previewer.
hints: Don't write your own parser; use a library like Marked.js via CDN.
stretch_goals: Add a 'Download as .md' button.

title: Memory Card Game
description: A classic grid-based memory matching game.
deliverable: A playable game with move counters, a timer, and a win state.
hints: Use CSS 3D transforms for the card flipping effect.
stretch_goals: Add multiple difficulty levels (grid sizes).

title: Expense Tracker
description: An app to log income and expenses, updating a running balance.
deliverable: A UI that categorizes inputs and visualizes cash flow.
hints: Use array `.reduce()` to quickly calculate total balances.
stretch_goals: Integrate Chart.js to show a pie chart of spending categories.

title: Interactive Quiz App
description: A multiple-choice quiz that pulls questions from the Open Trivia API.
deliverable: A dynamically generated quiz with score tracking.
hints: Handle the API response carefully to randomize the correct answer's position.
stretch_goals: Implement a leaderboard using Local Storage.

title: GitHub Profile Fetcher
description: An app that takes a GitHub username and displays their repositories, followers, and bio.
deliverable: A clean card UI populated by GitHub's public API data.
hints: Use `https://api.github.com/users/{username}`.
stretch_goals: Handle 404 errors gracefully with a 'User Not Found' UI state.

title: Phase Pages Layout Scaffold
description: Design the HTML/CSS template for the dedicated 'Phase Pages' where you will store commands and notes.
deliverable: A reusable layout featuring a sidebar navigation and a main content area.
hints: Plan for scalability. Ensure the sidebar collapses into a hamburger menu on mobile.
stretch_goals: Create CSS variables for easy theme swapping later.

title: Typing Speed Tester
description: An app that calculates Words Per Minute (WPM) and accuracy.
deliverable: A text input interface that highlights typing errors in real-time.
hints: Listen to the `input` event on a textarea to compare keystrokes against a target string.
stretch_goals: Implement a dynamic WPM line chart that updates as the user types.

title: Custom UI Component Library
description: Build a set of reusable JavaScript components (modals, accordions, tooltips) from scratch without libraries.
deliverable: A single page demonstrating various interactive UI elements.
hints: Focus on accessibility (ARIA roles) for these components.
stretch_goals: Package them into a single JS class so they can be easily instantiated.

title: Dynamic Data Filter
description: Build a filtering system that sorts a grid of items by categories (e.g., filtering projects by tech stack).
deliverable: A smooth UI that animates elements out of the DOM when filtered out.
hints: Use HTML `data-` attributes to tag elements for easy filtering in JS.
stretch_goals: Allow multi-category selection (e.g., filtering for both 'Python' AND 'Beginner').

title: PHASE CAPSTONE: The Second Brain Hub
description: Combine the portfolio scaffold, dynamic filtering, and phase pages into one cohesive, interactive dashboard hooked up to JSONBin.
deliverable: The finalized frontend shell for your entire 130-week marathon.
hints: This is where you implement the `fetch()` logic to pull this exact JSON file and render the cards dynamically.
stretch_goals: Add skeleton loading states while the JSON data is being fetched.

title: Python CLI Task Manager
description: Translate your web-based task manager into a pure Python command-line interface.
deliverable: A script that accepts arguments to add, list, and complete tasks, saving them to a local JSON file.
hints: Use Python's built-in `argparse` module to handle command-line inputs cleanly.
stretch_goals: Add colorized terminal output using a library like `colorama`.

title: Automated Web Scraper
description: Build a script that extracts article headlines and links from a news site or blog.
deliverable: A Python script that outputs scraped data into a structured CSV file.
hints: Always inspect the target website's `robots.txt` before scraping.
stretch_goals: Schedule the script to run daily and append new articles to the CSV without duplicating.

title: Basic Statistical Calculator
description: A script designed to parse an array of numbers and calculate the mean, median, and mode from scratch.
deliverable: A reusable math module.
hints: Don't use Pandas yet. Write the raw mathematical logic yourself to understand the core formulas.
stretch_goals: Handle edge cases, like bimodal datasets or empty inputs gracefully.

title: Advanced Stats & Variance Engine
description: Extend your statistical calculator to compute variance, standard deviation, skewness, and kurtosis.
deliverable: A robust CLI tool that outputs advanced descriptive statistics from a raw data input.
hints: Transition to using NumPy here. It is optimized for high-performance array operations.
stretch_goals: Generate a text-based histogram in the terminal to visualize the distribution curve.

title: Bulk Document Converter
description: A utility script that reads a directory of Markdown files and compiles them into properly formatted Microsoft Word documents.
deliverable: An automated pipeline for converting developer notes into shareable `.docx` formats.
hints: Map Markdown heading levels (`#`, `##`) directly to Microsoft Word heading styles.
stretch_goals: Add functionality to embed images referenced in the Markdown into the final Word document.

title: CSV Data Cleaner
description: Load a massive, messy dataset (like Kaggle Titanic data) and clean it by handling missing values and dropping duplicates.
deliverable: A script that outputs a pristine, analysis-ready CSV.
hints: Familiarize yourself with `df.dropna()` and `df.fillna()` methods in Pandas.
stretch_goals: Write a generic reporting function that prints out how many rows were dropped or altered.

title: Playlist Audio Features Extractor
description: Connect to the Spotify API to pull metadata and audio features (tempo, energy, acousticness) from a public playlist.
deliverable: A Pandas DataFrame populated with rich audio data.
hints: You will need to set up a developer account with Spotify to get your OAuth tokens.
stretch_goals: Rank the tracks based on specific audio metrics and export the top 10.

title: Data Visualizer Engine
description: Take the clean dataset from Week 21 or 22 and generate insightful charts (bar graphs, scatter plots, correlation matrices).
deliverable: A script that saves high-resolution `.png` graphs to a local folder.
hints: Seaborn makes complex statistical plots much easier to read than basic Matplotlib.
stretch_goals: Create a multi-plot figure that displays four different visual metrics on one canvas.

title: Automated Email Reporter
description: Write a script that attaches your generated data visualization graphs and a summary text file, then emails it securely via SMTP.
deliverable: A functioning email automation pipeline.
hints: Use an 'App Password' if using Gmail, as standard passwords will be blocked for security.
stretch_goals: Pull the email body text directly from an external database or API.

title: Weather Data Logger
description: A script that pings a weather API every hour and appends the temperature and conditions to an SQLite database.
deliverable: A lightweight, persistent local database tracking time-series data.
hints: Use DB Browser for SQLite to visually inspect your tables without writing queries.
stretch_goals: Write a script to query the database and find the average temperature over the last 24 hours.

title: Grid Pathfinding Logic (A* Prep)
description: Build the raw mathematical logic for an A* pathfinding algorithm that navigates a 2D array in the terminal.
deliverable: A pure Python script that calculates the shortest path from point A to point B around hardcoded obstacles.
hints: Focus entirely on the logic (Heuristics, Open Sets, Closed Sets) before worrying about visual graphics in C++ later.
stretch_goals: Print the grid to the terminal using ASCII characters to trace the final path.

title: Stock Price Visualizer
description: Pull historical stock data using the yfinance library and plot moving averages.
deliverable: A script that outputs a professional-looking financial chart with trend lines.
hints: Calculate a 50-day and 200-day Simple Moving Average (SMA) using Pandas' `.rolling()` method.
stretch_goals: Highlight sections on the graph where the 50-day SMA crosses the 200-day SMA.

title: Local File Organizer
description: A script that scans your 'Downloads' folder and automatically moves files into categorized folders based on their extensions (.pdf, .jpg, .docx).
deliverable: A clean, automated file management utility.
hints: Test this on a dummy folder first so you don't accidentally lose important files!
stretch_goals: Add a feature to log moved files in a `.txt` audit trail.

title: Simple Python Web API
description: Use Flask or FastAPI to serve your own custom data (like your 130-week project list) over a local network.
deliverable: A locally hosted API endpoint that returns JSON when visited in a browser.
hints: FastAPI is highly recommended here due to its automatic documentation generation (Swagger UI).
stretch_goals: Implement basic API key authentication for your endpoints.

title: API + Database Integration
description: Connect your new Web API from Week 29 to your SQLite database from Week 25.
deliverable: An API that allows users to execute GET requests to retrieve live database records.
hints: Ensure your SQL queries are parameterized to prevent SQL injection (good practice for Phase 9).
stretch_goals: Add POST routes to allow external data insertion into the database.

title: Sentiment Analysis Script
description: Use a basic NLP library to parse a block of text and determine if the sentiment is positive, negative, or neutral.
deliverable: A script that grades product reviews or tweets on a sentiment scale.
hints: TextBlob is an excellent wrapper for NLTK that makes sentiment scoring incredibly simple.
stretch_goals: Scrape live reviews from a website and analyze the overall sentiment of a product.

title: Interactive Data App (Streamlit)
description: Turn your Python data scripts into an interactive, browser-based web application with zero frontend code.
deliverable: A deployed Streamlit app where a user can adjust parameters via sliders to change the data output.
hints: Streamlit reruns the entire script top-to-bottom every time a user interaction occurs, so cache heavy data loads.
stretch_goals: Deploy the app publicly using Streamlit Community Cloud.

title: Automated EDA Profiler
description: Build a script that takes any raw CSV file and automatically generates a comprehensive HTML report of missing values, correlations, and distributions.
deliverable: A universal data profiling utility.
hints: This is essentially a 'magic' library that does heavy EDA visualization in a single line of code.
stretch_goals: Integrate this into your FastAPI server to allow users to upload CSVs via the web and receive a report.

title: Jupyter Notebook Masterclass
description: Take your most complex dataset so far and conduct a full Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) within a Jupyter Notebook, documenting every step in Markdown cells.
deliverable: A comprehensive `.ipynb` file that reads like a professional data science report.
hints: Focus heavily on the storytelling aspect. Data science is about explaining the numbers, not just calculating them.
stretch_goals: Export the finalized notebook as an interactive HTML file.

title: PHASE CAPSTONE: Statistical Dashboard Engine
description: Combine Streamlit, your advanced statistics calculators, and Matplotlib into one unified application. Allow a user to upload a raw CSV, run a full statistical breakdown, and output an interactive web report displaying the variance, standard deviation, skewness, kurtosis, and rich visual plots.
deliverable: A fully functional, interactive data science web application.
hints: Use Streamlit's `st.file_uploader` to accept dynamic user datasets.
Ensure you handle errors if a user uploads a CSV with non-numeric data.
stretch_goals: Add an export button that compiles the web dashboard into a downloadable PDF report.

title: C++ Fundamentals & I/O
description: Set up your C++ compiler and build a robust command-line calculator that can parse complex mathematical strings.
deliverable: A compiled CLI program that safely handles user input and mathematical order of operations.
hints: Focus on setting up a solid `CMakeLists.txt` file so compiling is easy moving forward.
stretch_goals: Add support for algebraic variables (e.g., storing 'x = 5').

title: Pointers & Memory Manager
description: Dive into manual memory management by building a custom Linked List data structure from scratch using raw pointers.
deliverable: A C++ program that can create, traverse, and safely delete a dynamic linked list without memory leaks.
hints: Always pair every `new` keyword with a `delete` keyword.
Use tools like Valgrind to check for memory leaks.
stretch_goals: Convert the singly linked list into a doubly linked list.

title: Sorting Algorithm Benchmarker
description: Implement Bubble Sort, Merge Sort, and Quick Sort. Generate an array of 10,000 random integers and time how long each takes to execute.
deliverable: A CLI tool that outputs exact execution times (in milliseconds) for different algorithms.
hints: Use the `<chrono>` library for highly precise timing.
stretch_goals: Export the timing results into a CSV file for your Python tools to analyze later.

title: Text-Based Maze Generator
description: Use a Depth-First Search (DFS) algorithm to procedurally generate a random, solvable ASCII maze in the terminal.
deliverable: A script that outputs a unique maze layout using '#' for walls and ' ' for paths.
hints: Represent the maze grid as a 2D array or a 1D vector with calculated indices.
stretch_goals: Ensure the generator guarantees at least one path from the top-left to the bottom-right.

title: A* Pathfinding Implementation
description: Translate the Python A* logic you wrote in Week 26 into highly optimized, compiled C++ code.
deliverable: A function that takes the maze from Week 39 and prints the shortest path to solve it.
hints: Use `std::priority_queue` to manage your open set efficiently.
stretch_goals: Allow diagonal movement with a higher movement cost.

title: A* Visualizer (SFML)
description: Hook your C++ A* logic up to the SFML multimedia library to render the algorithm solving the maze visually in a 2D window.
deliverable: A compiled desktop window displaying the algorithm searching and finding the path in real-time.
hints: SFML setup can be tricky. Take your time configuring the linker settings in your IDE.
stretch_goals: Allow the user to draw walls on the grid using their mouse before starting the algorithm.

title: Basic Game Loop & State Machine
description: Write a pure C++ game loop (Update, Render, Process Input) and a State Machine to switch between 'Main Menu', 'Playing', and 'Game Over'.
deliverable: A terminal application that perfectly loops and handles state transitions without crashing.
hints: Use `enum class` to strictly define your game states.
stretch_goals: Implement a 'Pause' state that pauses the logic but keeps the loop running.

title: JSON Save State System
description: Integrate a C++ JSON library to serialize game variables (health, gold, level) and write them to a local file, then read them back on boot.
deliverable: A robust save/load system for application data.
hints: The `nlohmann/json` library is the industry standard for modern C++. It's a single header file, making it easy to include.
stretch_goals: Encrypt the save file using a basic XOR cipher to prevent save-scumming.

title: CLI Inventory Manager
description: Build an object-oriented inventory system using C++ Vectors and Structs. Allow adding, dropping, and inspecting items.
deliverable: A text interface where a user can manage a limited-slot inventory.
hints: Use `std::vector` for the inventory. It dynamically resizes and is much safer than raw arrays.
stretch_goals: Implement weight limits. An item can't be added if it exceeds max carry weight.

title: PHASE CAPSTONE: 'Freserve'
description: Combine the Game Loop, State Machine, Save System, and Inventory Manager into a complete text-based resource management survival game called 'Freserve'.
deliverable: A fully compiled, playable C++ game where the user manages resources, explores zones, and progresses through days while saving their state.
hints: Design your class hierarchy on paper before writing code. Player -> Inventory -> Item.
stretch_goals: Add random events (e.g., 'A storm destroyed 5 wood') that trigger between turns based on RNG.

title: Java OOP & Banking App
description: Transition to Java by building a strict Object-Oriented console banking application with Encapsulation (private balances, public deposit/withdraw methods).
deliverable: A Java console app that simulates accounts and secure transactions.
hints: Get comfortable with the `public static void main(String[] args)` entry point.
stretch_goals: Implement an overdraft penalty feature.

title: RPG Inheritance System
description: Use Inheritance and Polymorphism to design an RPG class system. Base class `Character`, derived classes `Warrior`, `Mage`, `Rogue`, each overriding an `attack()` method.
deliverable: A script demonstrating dynamic method dispatch based on object type.
hints: Use the `@Override` annotation to ensure you are correctly replacing the parent method.
stretch_goals: Create a battle simulator that puts an array of mixed characters against a Boss object.

title: Abstract Shapes & Interfaces
description: Learn the difference between Interfaces and Abstract Classes by building a rigid geometry calculator (Area, Perimeter) for different shape objects.
deliverable: A clean, extensible geometry library.
hints: Interfaces define *what* a class can do (e.g., `Drawable`), Abstract classes define *what a class is* (e.g., `Shape`).
stretch_goals: Add a custom Exception if a shape is instantiated with invalid dimensions (like negative radius).

title: Secure Log Parser
description: Read a massive server log file. Use Try/Catch blocks and Exception Handling to gracefully skip corrupted lines without crashing the program.
deliverable: A file parsing utility that generates a clean summary report of warnings and errors.
hints: Use Java's `BufferedReader` for efficient large file reading.
stretch_goals: Use Regex (Regular Expressions) to extract specific IP addresses from the logs.

title: Custom HashMap Implementation
description: Don't use `java.util.HashMap`. Build your own Hash Table data structure using an array of linked lists to handle collisions.
deliverable: A functional Key-Value store mapped entirely from scratch.
hints: Understand the modulo operator `%` to map large hash codes to small array indices.
stretch_goals: Implement an automatic resizing function when the load factor gets too high.

title: Multithreading & Race Conditions
description: Create a program where two threads try to withdraw money from the same bank account simultaneously. Then, fix the bug using the `synchronized` keyword.
deliverable: A simulation proving thread-safety issues and their solutions.
hints: Extend the `Thread` class or implement the `Runnable` interface.
stretch_goals: Use a Thread Pool (`ExecutorService`) to handle 100 simultaneous transactions.

title: Design Patterns: Singleton & Factory
description: Implement the Singleton pattern for a `DatabaseConnection` class, and a Factory pattern to generate UI buttons dynamically.
deliverable: A codebase demonstrating enterprise-level architectural patterns.
hints: A Singleton ensures a class only ever has one instance (useful for game managers or database links).
stretch_goals: Implement the Builder pattern for constructing complex objects step-by-step.

title: Design Patterns: Weather Station (Observer)
description: Build a weather station core object that automatically updates multiple display modules (Current, Stats, Forecast) using the Observer pattern.
deliverable: A decoupled event-driven system.
hints: The Subject maintains a list of Observers. When state changes, it loops through and calls `update()` on all of them.
stretch_goals: Allow display modules to dynamically subscribe and unsubscribe from the weather station at runtime.

title: JavaFX Desktop GUI
description: Transition from the console to a real desktop application. Build a modern UI for a To-Do list using JavaFX and FXML.
deliverable: A compiled windowed application with buttons, lists, and text fields.
hints: Use Scene Builder to drag-and-drop your UI layout instead of coding the XML by hand.
stretch_goals: Bind CSS stylesheets to your JavaFX app for a modern, flat-design look.

title: JDBC SQLite Integration
description: Connect your Java code to a local SQLite database using Java Database Connectivity (JDBC). Perform CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete).
deliverable: A Java application that securely queries and modifies persistent database tables.
hints: You will need to download the SQLite JDBC driver `.jar` file and add it to your classpath.
stretch_goals: Implement the DAO (Data Access Object) pattern to separate your SQL queries from your main logic.

title: Socket Chat Server
description: Build a multithreaded network server using `java.net.Socket` that can accept multiple client connections simultaneously and broadcast messages to all of them.
deliverable: A headless server application listening on a specific port.
hints: Each client connection needs to be handed off to its own separate Thread so the server isn't blocked from accepting new connections.
stretch_goals: Implement server-side commands (like `/kick user`).

title: Socket Chat Client GUI
description: Build the JavaFX frontend for the chat server you made in Week 56. Connect over `localhost` and send/receive messages.
deliverable: A functional desktop chat application (like a very simple Discord).
hints: Updating JavaFX UI elements from a background network thread will crash the app. Use `Platform.runLater()` to safely update the UI.
stretch_goals: Add timestamps and usernames to the chat bubbles.

title: Unit Testing with JUnit
description: Learn Test-Driven Development (TDD) by building a complex Math/String utility library and writing JUnit tests for every single method before you write the code.
deliverable: A robust test suite that turns green when your code passes all edge cases.
hints: Think of edge cases: What happens if a string is null? What if an integer is MAX_VALUE?
stretch_goals: Integrate Mockito to mock out external database calls during testing.

title: Build Tools: Maven / Gradle
description: Stop compiling via the command line or IDE buttons. Restructure an existing project to use Maven, managing your dependencies (like SQLite and JUnit) via a `pom.xml` file.
deliverable: A standardized enterprise-ready project structure.
hints: Maven automatically handles downloading `.jar` files so you never have to manually add them to your classpath again.
stretch_goals: Create a Maven lifecycle hook to automatically run your JUnit tests every time you build a `.jar`.

title: PHASE CAPSTONE: Enterprise Inventory Desktop App
description: Combine JavaFX, JDBC (SQLite), Multithreading, and Design Patterns into a massive desktop application. Build a Point-of-Sale/Inventory manager with a beautiful UI, persistent database, and a full JUnit test suite built with Maven.
deliverable: A professional-grade desktop application ready for an enterprise portfolio.
hints: Use the DAO design pattern to cleanly separate your database SQL commands from your JavaFX UI code.
stretch_goals: Implement a background thread that automatically checks stock levels every 5 minutes and triggers a UI alert if an item is low.

title: Android Studio & UI Layouts
description: Set up the Android Studio environment and build a responsive calculator UI using XML ConstraintLayout.
deliverable: An Android emulator running a non-functional but perfectly scaled calculator interface.
hints: Spend time learning how ConstraintLayout anchors work. It will save you hours of UI headaches later.
stretch_goals: Support both portrait and landscape orientations with different XML layouts.

title: Interactive State & Event Listeners
description: Wire up your calculator UI to actual Java logic. Handle button clicks, update TextViews, and manage the app's state.
deliverable: A fully functional calculator app running on your physical phone or emulator.
hints: Use `findViewById()` to link your XML elements to your Java objects.
stretch_goals: Add a 'history' pane that shows the last 5 calculations.

title: Multi-Screen Navigation (Intents)
description: Build a Quiz App with multiple screens (Start Menu, Quiz Screen, Results Screen). Use Intents to pass the final score between Activities.
deliverable: An app that successfully navigates forward and backward without crashing or losing data.
hints: Understand the Activity Lifecycle. What happens to your variables when the user minimizes the app?
stretch_goals: Use `putExtra()` to send the user's name from the first screen to the results screen.

title: Dynamic Lists with RecyclerView
description: Build a Contacts or Inventory app. Use RecyclerView and a custom Adapter to display a scrollable list of hundreds of items efficiently.
deliverable: A smooth, scrollable list UI that recycles memory off-screen.
hints: RecyclerView is notoriously tricky the first time. Follow the boilerplate code carefully (ViewHolder, Adapter, LayoutManager).
stretch_goals: Implement a swipe-to-delete feature using ItemTouchHelper.

title: Local Storage (SharedPreferences)
description: Create a Habit Tracker app that saves user preferences (like Dark Mode) and daily streaks using Android's built-in SharedPreferences.
deliverable: An app that remembers user data even after it is completely closed and reopened.
hints: SharedPreferences is for lightweight Key-Value pairs only, not massive datasets.
stretch_goals: Save a serialized JSON string into SharedPreferences to store a complex object.

title: Fetching APIs in Android (Retrofit)
description: Build a Movie Explorer app that fetches live data from the TMDB API using the Retrofit library and displays the movie posters in a grid.
deliverable: A networked app that successfully requests, parses, and displays JSON data from the web.
hints: Don't forget to add the `<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />` tag in your Manifest!
stretch_goals: Add a search bar that queries the API dynamically as the user types.

title: Persistent Data with Room Database
description: Upgrade your Habit Tracker from Week 65. Use the Room persistence library (Android's SQLite wrapper) to store complex, structured logs.
deliverable: An app with a fully functioning local database, utilizing DAOs (Data Access Objects).
hints: Room queries cannot run on the main UI thread. You must use background threads (Executors or Coroutines if using Kotlin).
stretch_goals: Implement a query that returns the user's longest historical habit streak.

title: Background Services & Notifications
description: Build a timer app that continues counting down in a Background Service even when minimized, pushing a System Notification when finished.
deliverable: A foreground service that safely interacts with Android's notification channels.
hints: Android heavily restricts background services to save battery. Use a Foreground Service for a timer.
stretch_goals: Add action buttons directly onto the notification (e.g., 'Stop Timer').

title: Device Hardware & Permissions
description: Build an audio recorder. Request runtime permissions from the user, access the device's microphone, and save an audio file to the local file system.
deliverable: An app that successfully navigates Android's strict runtime permission dialogs and utilizes hardware sensors.
hints: Since Android 6.0, you can't just declare dangerous permissions in the Manifest; you must ask the user via a popup.
stretch_goals: Visualize the audio waveform dynamically on the screen while recording.

title: PHASE CAPSTONE: 'Bandi' Voice Assistant (v1)
description: Combine the microphone permissions, Intents, and the native Android SpeechRecognizer API to build Bandi, a voice-controlled assistant. Speak commands to set timers, open other apps, or fetch the weather.
deliverable: A highly interactive, voice-activated Android application.
hints: Use the native TextToSpeech engine so Bandi can actually talk back to you.
Keep the command parsing simple for now (e.g., checking if the speech contains the word 'weather').
stretch_goals: Implement a wake-word listener (requires a background service).

title: Scikit-Learn & Linear Regression
description: Shift back to Python. Use Scikit-Learn to train a Linear Regression model that predicts housing prices based on square footage and bedrooms.
deliverable: A Python script that loads CSV data, trains a model, and outputs a prediction with a calculated Mean Squared Error.
hints: Always split your data into a 'Training Set' and a 'Test Set' using `train_test_split`.
stretch_goals: Plot the line of best fit over a scatter plot of your data using Matplotlib.

title: Logistic Regression & Classification
description: Linear Regression is for continuous numbers. Use Logistic Regression to classify binary outcomes (e.g., predicting if a patient has a disease based on health metrics: Yes/No).
deliverable: A classification model evaluated using a Confusion Matrix.
hints: A Confusion Matrix shows True Positives, True Negatives, False Positives, and False Negatives.
stretch_goals: Calculate and print the Precision, Recall, and F1-Score of your model.

title: Decision Trees & Random Forests
description: Build a Decision Tree model to classify a more complex dataset (like the classic Iris flower dataset). Then, upgrade it to a Random Forest to prevent overfitting.
deliverable: A robust ensemble model that outperforms a single decision tree.
hints: A Random Forest is essentially hundreds of Decision Trees voting on the best answer.
stretch_goals: Export a visual graph of one of the Decision Trees to a `.png` file.

title: K-Means Clustering (Unsupervised)
description: Take an unlabelled dataset of customer purchases and use K-Means Clustering to automatically discover hidden market segments.
deliverable: A script that groups data points into 'K' clusters without knowing what the groups represent beforehand.
hints: Use the 'Elbow Method' to mathematically determine the optimal number for K.
stretch_goals: Create a 3D scatter plot of your clusters using Matplotlib's `mplot3d` toolkit.

title: Natural Language Processing (NLP)
description: Process a dataset of 10,000 movie reviews. Clean the text, remove stop words, and convert the sentences into a TF-IDF mathematical matrix.
deliverable: A text processing pipeline that prepares raw English sentences for machine learning.
hints: TF-IDF stands for Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency. It weighs how important a word is to a document.
stretch_goals: Implement Lemmatization (converting 'running' and 'ran' into the base word 'run').

title: Spam Classifier Engine
description: Combine the NLP pipeline from Week 75 with a Naive Bayes classifier to build a highly accurate Email/SMS Spam detector.
deliverable: A CLI tool where you can type a custom sentence and the model instantly flags it as 'Spam' or 'Ham'.
hints: Naive Bayes is remarkably fast and effective for text classification tasks.
stretch_goals: Pickle (serialize) the trained model to a `.pkl` file so you don't have to retrain it every time you run the script.

title: Intro to Deep Learning (TensorFlow/PyTorch)
description: Set up your deep learning environment. Build a simple Feedforward Neural Network to solve the classic MNIST dataset (identifying handwritten digits 0-9).
deliverable: A trained neural network capable of recognizing handwritten numbers with over 90% accuracy.
hints: Pick either TensorFlow or PyTorch and stick with it. PyTorch is generally favored by researchers, while TensorFlow/Keras is great for beginners and deployment.
stretch_goals: Write a script that allows you to draw a digit in a desktop window and pass it to the model for real-time prediction.

title: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
description: Standard networks flatten images. Use a CNN to analyze spatial grids, building an image classifier that distinguishes between Cats and Dogs.
deliverable: A deep learning model utilizing Convolutional and MaxPooling layers.
hints: Training images takes time. If you don't have a dedicated GPU, consider using Google Colab for free GPU access during this week.
stretch_goals: Implement Data Augmentation (flipping, rotating training images) to increase model accuracy.

title: Transfer Learning (MobileNet)
description: Don't train from scratch. Take a massive, pre-trained model like MobileNet, freeze its base layers, and retrain only the final layer on your custom image dataset.
deliverable: A highly accurate, lightweight image classifier trained in a fraction of the time.
hints: MobileNet is specifically optimized to be small and fast, making it perfect for mobile devices (like Bandi!).
stretch_goals: Export the final model into the TensorFlow Lite (`.tflite`) format.

title: Deploying Models as APIs
description: Take your Spam Classifier (Week 76) or Image Classifier (Week 79) and wrap it in a FastAPI server. Allow external applications to send data and receive predictions.
deliverable: A functional REST API hosting a live Machine Learning model.
hints: Load the `.pkl` or `.h5` model once when the API starts up, not inside the route function itself, to ensure fast response times.
stretch_goals: Deploy the API to a free cloud service like Render or Heroku.

title: Sequence Models (RNN / LSTM)
description: Learn how models handle sequential data like time or text. Train an LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) network to predict the next word in a sentence or forecast stock prices.
deliverable: A recurrent neural network capable of processing sequences of varying lengths.
hints: LSTMs solve the 'vanishing gradient' problem, allowing the network to remember context from earlier in a sequence.
stretch_goals: Build a simple text-generation script that writes a paragraph in the style of Shakespeare based on your trained model.

title: Open Source LLMs (Hugging Face)
description: Skip training your own text models. Learn how to interface with the Hugging Face `transformers` library to download and run small, powerful, pre-trained AI models locally.
deliverable: A local Python script utilizing an open-source model (like a quantized LLaMA or BERT) to answer questions or summarize text.
hints: Look into 'Quantized' models (like GGUF format). They drastically reduce the RAM required to run the model locally.
stretch_goals: Wrap this Hugging Face model in your FastAPI server from Week 80.

title: On-Device ML (TensorFlow Lite)
description: Learn the pipeline for optimizing models for edge devices. Convert your custom classifiers into the ultra-efficient `.tflite` format and test them on dummy data.
deliverable: A highly compressed model ready for mobile integration.
hints: Mobile devices don't have standard Python environments. TFLite bridges the gap between Python training and Java/C++ execution.
stretch_goals: Use Post-Training Quantization to shrink the model size by converting 32-bit floats to 8-bit integers.

title: Audio Processing & ML
description: Process audio files. Convert `.wav` files into spectrograms (visual representations of sound) and train a lightweight CNN to classify different spoken wake-words.
deliverable: A model capable of recognizing a specific word (like 'Hey Bandi') from raw audio data.
hints: The `librosa` library is standard for extracting audio features like MFCCs (Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients).
stretch_goals: Export this wake-word model to TFLite.

title: PHASE CAPSTONE: 'Bandi' Smart Assistant (v2)
description: Replace the basic rules in your Bandi Android app with actual ML logic. Integrate either a local TFLite model or connect the app via Retrofit to your FastAPI server running a Hugging Face NLP model.
deliverable: An Android smart assistant that processes natural language using real machine learning logic.
hints: If running the model on a server via API, ensure your app displays a 'thinking' UI state while waiting for the network response.
Combining your Android code from Week 70 with your ML code from Week 82 is the ultimate full-stack achievement.
stretch_goals: Integrate the TFLite wake-word model from Week 84 directly into an Android background service.

title: Godot Basics & Character Controller
description: Set up Godot and build a 2D CharacterBody that can move, jump, and apply gravity using GDScript.
deliverable: A playable 2D square that moves smoothly across the screen with proper physics.
hints: Use the `_physics_process(delta)` function for anything involving movement and gravity to keep it tied to the framerate.
stretch_goals: Implement a 'coyote time' jump (allowing the player to jump a few frames after walking off a ledge).

title: Collisions & TileMaps
description: Design a basic level using Godot's TileMap system and ensure your character collides correctly with walls and floors.
deliverable: A traversable 2D platformer level.
hints: Set up collision layers and masks properly. Player should be on Layer 1, World on Layer 2.
stretch_goals: Add an 'Ice' tile material that changes the player's friction when walked on.

title: Signals & UI Integration
description: Build a Health Bar UI. Use Godot's Signal system to emit an event whenever the player takes damage, updating the UI automatically.
deliverable: A decoupled system where game logic updates interface elements without hardcoding.
hints: Signals are Godot's version of the Observer pattern you built in Java during Week 53!
stretch_goals: Animate the health bar so it smoothly drains instead of snapping instantly.

title: AnimationPlayer & Sprite Sheets
description: Import a 2D sprite sheet and use the AnimationPlayer node to create Idle, Run, and Jump animations mapped to your player's state.
deliverable: A visually expressive character that reacts to player input.
hints: Flip the sprite's `flip_h` property in code when changing directions left or right.
stretch_goals: Add a dust particle effect (CPUParticles2D) that triggers when the player lands a jump.

title: Enemy AI & State Machines
description: Create an enemy that patrols between two points and switches to a 'Chase' state when the player enters its Detection Area.
deliverable: An interactive NPC with basic decision-making logic.
hints: Use an Area2D node with a circular collision shape to represent the enemy's line of sight.
stretch_goals: Add a simple attack animation when the enemy gets within striking distance.

title: Save/Load Data in Godot
description: Implement a system that serializes the player's position, health, and level into a JSON file, and can deserialize it to load the game.
deliverable: A persistent save state system native to your Godot project.
hints: Use Godot's `user://` path to save files safely, avoiding read-only errors on exported games.
stretch_goals: Create a Main Menu screen with a 'Continue Game' button.

title: Audio Management & Polish
description: Create a centralized Audio Manager (Singleton/Autoload) that handles background music and sound effects without cutting out when scenes change.
deliverable: A polished auditory experience with volume control.
hints: Autoloads (Singletons) are nodes that persist across the entire runtime of the game, making them perfect for background music.
stretch_goals: Implement a settings menu that adjusts separate Audio Buses (Master, Music, SFX).

title: Stepping into 3D: Environments
description: Leave 2D behind. Set up a 3D Godot scene, implement a first-person Camera3D, and build a basic room using CSG (Constructive Solid Geometry) nodes.
deliverable: A navigable 3D environment.
hints: CSG nodes are fantastic for rapid prototyping levels without needing to import models from Blender.
stretch_goals: Add a DirectionalLight3D and enable shadows.

title: 3D Raycasting & Interactions
description: Use a RayCast3D node attached to your first-person camera to detect when the player is looking at an interactable object (like a door or a switch).
deliverable: A functional interaction system for 3D worlds.
hints: Ensure your interactable objects are on a specific collision layer so the RayCast only detects them and ignores standard walls.
stretch_goals: Change the player's crosshair UI when looking at an interactable object.

title: Intro to Shader Programming
description: Write a custom visual shader (using Godot's visual shader editor or raw GLSL syntax) to create an animated, glowing water texture or a forcefield effect.
deliverable: A visually impressive, GPU-rendered material.
hints: Shaders run directly on the graphics card and manipulate the color or position of every single pixel/vertex simultaneously.
stretch_goals: Make the water shader interact with the `TIME` variable so it ripples endlessly.

title: Exporting for Web (HTML5)
description: Learn the export pipeline. Take your polished 2D platformer from earlier and export it as an HTML5 package. Test it locally in your browser.
deliverable: A standalone game that runs natively in a web browser without any installation.
hints: You will need to install Godot's export templates. Make sure to choose 'Web' as your target platform.
stretch_goals: Embed the exported game directly into a custom HTML frame on your 130-week GitHub Pages site.

title: Godot REST API Integration
description: Use Godot's HTTPRequest node to send and receive JSON data from the internet (e.g., fetching a 'Message of the Day' from a public API).
deliverable: A game that dynamically updates its content from a live web server.
hints: Godot processes HTTP requests asynchronously. You must connect the `request_completed` signal to handle the data when it finally arrives.
stretch_goals: Post dummy high score data to your JSONBin API from Week 1.

title: Procedural Level Generation
description: Write an algorithm that uses Godot's FastNoiseLite or random walkers to procedurally generate a dungeon layout or terrain map at runtime.
deliverable: A game where every playthrough yields a completely different level.
hints: Start simple: randomly place 'room' nodes and connect them with corridors using your A* logic from Phase 3.
stretch_goals: Add an RNG 'seed' input so users can share and replay specific generation layouts.

title: Juice & Game Feel
description: Take an existing project and add 'juice': screen shake, particle explosions on death, hit-stop (briefly pausing time on impact), and dynamic lighting.
deliverable: A game that feels professional, snappy, and deeply satisfying to play.
hints: Use Godot's `Tween` system to smoothly interpolate properties (like scaling up an enemy when hit).
stretch_goals: Implement a chromatic aberration shader that triggers briefly during large explosions.

resources.name: Game Feel (Juice it or lose it)

title: PHASE CAPSTONE: Web-Exported Action Game
description: Combine movement, AI, shaders, juice, and audio into a polished, 5-minute arcade experience. Export it to WebAssembly and host it live on your GitHub Pages portfolio.
deliverable: A complete, portfolio-ready browser game.
hints: Scope incredibly small. One solid level that feels great is vastly superior to 10 buggy, unfinished levels.
stretch_goals: Add a global leaderboard system using HTTPRequest (which you will build the backend for in Phase 8).

title: Node.js & Express Server
description: Set up a fundamental Node.js backend using the Express framework. Create basic GET and POST routes that return JSON data.
deliverable: A local web server running on port 3000.
hints: Use Postman or ThunderClient (VS Code Extension) to test your API routes without needing a frontend.
stretch_goals: Implement an error-handling middleware that catches unknown routes (404s).

title: NoSQL Database Integration (MongoDB)
description: Connect your Express server to a free MongoDB Atlas cluster using Mongoose. Define a schema for a 'User' and perform CRUD operations.
deliverable: A cloud-connected API capable of reading and writing permanent data.
hints: Never commit your MongoDB connection string (URI) to GitHub. Use a `.env` file to store environment variables.
stretch_goals: Implement data validation in your Mongoose schema (e.g., ensuring an email string is formatted correctly).

title: User Authentication (JWT & bcrypt)
description: Build a secure registration and login system. Hash user passwords with bcrypt before saving them to MongoDB, and issue JSON Web Tokens (JWT) for session management.
deliverable: A secure, stateless authentication pipeline.
hints: A JWT is essentially a digital VIP pass. The server signs it, hands it to the user, and checks the signature on future requests.
stretch_goals: Create a protected route that can only be accessed if a valid JWT is provided in the Authorization header.

title: Image Uploads to Cloudinary
description: Implement a route that allows users to upload a profile picture. Use Multer to handle the incoming file and upload it to a free Cloudinary CDN, storing the image URL in MongoDB.
deliverable: An API capable of handling and routing binary file data securely.
hints: APIs usually handle JSON text. To handle files, your client must send data as `multipart/form-data`.
stretch_goals: Resize the image on the server using a library like Sharp before sending it to Cloudinary to save bandwidth.

title: Real-Time Comms with WebSockets
description: Standard APIs require the client to ask for data. WebSockets allow the server to push data instantly. Build a real-time global chat room using Socket.io.
deliverable: A server that broadcasts messages instantly to all connected clients.
hints: WebSockets run alongside your Express server but handle connections entirely differently. Think of it as an open phone line.
stretch_goals: Implement private 'rooms' so users can chat in isolated channels.

title: API Security & Rate Limiting
description: Protect your API from abuse. Implement Helmet.js to secure HTTP headers, CORS to restrict domain access, and a Rate Limiter to prevent spam requests.
deliverable: A hardened, production-ready backend.
hints: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) will block your GitHub Pages site from talking to your server unless you explicitly whitelist the GitHub domain.
stretch_goals: Set up logging with Morgan or Winston to track all incoming requests to a server log file.

title: Containerization with Docker Basics
description: Write a `Dockerfile` that packages your Node.js application and its environment into an isolated container, ensuring it runs identically on any machine.
deliverable: A dockerized web server.
hints: Containers solve the 'It works on my machine!' problem forever.
stretch_goals: Use `docker-compose` to spin up your Node server and a local MongoDB database instance simultaneously.

title: Backend Deployment (Render/Heroku)
description: Take your fully secured, database-connected API and deploy it live to the cloud so your GitHub Pages portfolio can communicate with it globally.
deliverable: A live, publicly accessible API endpoint.
hints: Ensure you configure your Environment Variables (like your MongoDB URI and JWT Secret) directly inside your hosting provider's dashboard.
stretch_goals: Set up an automated CI/CD pipeline so the server redeploys automatically when you push code to GitHub.

title: Cross-Platform Dashboard UI
description: Build a sleek frontend dashboard (HTML/CSS/JS) specifically designed to consume, display, and interact with the data from your newly deployed Node.js API.
deliverable: The visual interface for your backend services.
hints: Fetch the JWT token during login and store it in `localStorage`, then attach it to the headers of all subsequent API requests.
stretch_goals: Add pagination to your API and dashboard so you only load 10 items at a time.

title: PHASE CAPSTONE: Global High-Score Network
description: The ultimate integration. Retrofit your Godot game (Week 100) or Android app (Bandi) to send score/usage data to your live Node.js backend. Display that global data dynamically on your GitHub Pages portfolio.
deliverable: A complete ecosystem: a game, a server, a database, and a web dashboard, all communicating flawlessly in real-time.
hints: Double-check your CORS policies on the server to ensure both the Godot Web Export and your main site have permission to push/pull data.
stretch_goals: Implement a Socket.io feed on the website that flashes whenever a new high score is achieved globally in real-time.

title: Network Packet Sniffer
description: Use Python and the Scapy library to capture live network traffic and inspect the contents of unencrypted HTTP packets.
deliverable: A script that outputs the source and destination IPs of traffic on your local network.
hints: You will likely need to run this script with Administrator/Sudo privileges to access the network interface card.
stretch_goals: Filter the output to only show packets containing POST requests (where passwords might be submitted).

title: Python Port Scanner
description: Build a custom command-line tool that scans a target IP address for open ports to identify potential entry points.
deliverable: A CLI tool that takes an IP address and outputs a list of open ports and services.
hints: Use multi-threading so it doesn't take hours to scan all 65,535 ports.
stretch_goals: Grab the banner of the open port to detect if it's an SSH or HTTP server.

title: Offline Password Cracker
description: Write a script that takes a leaked SHA-256 hash and compares it against a common password dictionary file (like rockyou.txt) to crack it.
deliverable: A dictionary brute-force tool.
hints: You are just hashing every word in the dictionary file and seeing if the resulting string matches the stolen hash.
stretch_goals: Implement a 'Salt' parameter to see how salted hashes defeat basic dictionary attacks.

title: Directory Fuzzer (DirBuster Clone)
description: Build a tool that brute-forces hidden directories on a web server (e.g., trying `/admin`, `/backup`, `/test`) and logs the HTTP status codes.
deliverable: A script that discovers unlinked pages on a target website.
hints: Only test this against a local Node.js server you own! Scanning external sites without permission is illegal.
stretch_goals: Add an argument to change the user-agent string to spoof a mobile browser.

title: SQL Injection (SQLi) Tester
description: Write a Python script that automatically appends common SQL injection payloads (like `' OR 1=1 --`) to a website's URL parameters and checks the response for database errors.
deliverable: An automated vulnerability scanner for databases.
hints: Look for words like 'Syntax error' or 'MySQL' in the HTML response text to verify the injection worked.
stretch_goals: Extract the database version name automatically from a vulnerable endpoint.

title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Scanner
description: Create a script that submits safe JavaScript payloads (`<script>alert(1)</script>`) into forms on a local server to see if the input is sanitized.
deliverable: An automated form tester that identifies potential XSS vulnerabilities.
hints: If your payload reflects directly back in the HTML response without being escaped (e.g., converted to `<script>`), it's vulnerable.
stretch_goals: Test for Stored XSS by submitting a payload and then checking a completely different page (like a comments section) to see if it executed.

title: Subdomain Enumeration Tool
description: Build an OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) tool that queries public DNS records to map out hidden subdomains of a target company (e.g., `dev.example.com`).
deliverable: A reconnaissance script that expands an attack surface map.
hints: You can use the `dnspython` library to attempt zone transfers, or query free APIs like crt.sh.
stretch_goals: Export the discovered subdomains into a clean HTML report.

title: Malware Sandbox Simulation
description: Write a harmless 'Ransomware Simulator' in C++ that traverses a specific dummy folder, encrypts all the `.txt` files using XOR encryption, and drops a 'readme' note.
deliverable: A compiled executable demonstrating how cryptographic malware behaves on a system.
hints: Triple-check your file path logic. Hardcode the target to a specific testing folder so you don't accidentally encrypt your actual projects.
stretch_goals: Write the decryption function that restores the files when a key is provided.

title: API JWT Cracker
description: Extract a JSON Web Token (JWT) from a web request, decode its payload, and write a script that attempts to brute-force the server's secret signing key.
deliverable: A script that demonstrates why weak JWT secrets lead to total authentication bypass.
hints: You don't need to attack the server to crack a JWT; the cracking happens entirely offline on your own machine.
stretch_goals: Modify the JWT payload to elevate privileges (e.g., `"admin": true`) and sign it with the cracked key.

title: PHASE CAPSTONE: Automated Penetration Report Tool
description: Combine your Port Scanner, DirBuster, and SQLi tools into one master script. Point it at a local server, run it, and output a professional, formatted PDF security report.
deliverable: An automated vulnerability scanner that generates client-ready deliverables.
hints: Use the `reportlab` library in Python to dynamically generate the PDF structure.
stretch_goals: Assign a severity score (Low, Medium, High, Critical) to each vulnerability found.

title: Ecosystem Architecture & UML
description: No code this week. Design the architecture for your ultimate platform (e.g., a massive habit-tracking RPG ecosystem). Draw database schemas, API routes, and wireframes.
deliverable: A comprehensive project design document detailing how Android, Web, and ML will interact.
hints: Identify potential bottlenecks early. If your game pings the database every 60 frames, your server will crash.
stretch_goals: Write user stories and acceptance criteria for the core features.

title: Core Node.js API & Database
description: Build the central nervous system. Set up the Express API, MongoDB cluster, user authentication, and secure endpoints for your ecosystem.
deliverable: A deployed, hardened backend API handling core user logic.
hints: Apply the security middleware (Helmet, Rate Limiting) immediately, not as an afterthought.
stretch_goals: Implement a password reset flow using nodemailer.

title: Machine Learning Integration
description: Train a model for your ecosystem (e.g., predicting when a user is most likely to break their habit streak based on past data) and host it via FastAPI or directly in Node.
deliverable: An AI microservice that the main backend can query for intelligent insights.
hints: Keep the ML server separate from the main Node server so intensive calculations don't block basic API requests.
stretch_goals: Dockerize the ML API for easier cloud deployment.

title: Web Dashboard Frontend
description: Build the browser interface. Allow users to register, log in, and view their personalized data and ML insights on an interactive HTML/CSS/JS dashboard.
deliverable: A responsive frontend application communicating securely with your Node API.
hints: Ensure the dashboard gracefully handles API errors (like if the server is offline).
stretch_goals: Use Chart.js to visualize the user's progress and the ML predictions.

title: Native Android Client
description: Build the mobile counterpart. Use Retrofit to connect an Android application to the exact same Node.js database, keeping data synced between phone and web.
deliverable: A polished mobile app offering native ecosystem access.
hints: Use SharedPreferences to store the JWT so the user stays logged in between app launches.
stretch_goals: Integrate a native Android background service to send push notifications when the ML model predicts a streak loss.

title: Godot Game Client
description: Build a small Godot game that acts as a client. Use Godot's HTTPRequest node so users can log in, play the game, and have their high scores posted to the central ecosystem database.
deliverable: A playable game connected to a global cloud backend.
hints: Parse the JSON response from your login route to capture the JWT and attach it to the `Authorization` header of your score-posting requests.
stretch_goals: Export the Godot game to HTML5 and embed it directly into your Web Dashboard.

title: Security Audit & Penetration Test
description: Switch hats. Use the tools you built in Phase 9 to ruthlessly attack your Ultimate ecosystem. Attempt SQL injection, test JWT cracking, and fuzz your APIs.
deliverable: A multi-page vulnerability assessment report detailing the flaws in your own system.
hints: Be thorough. Try to bypass your Android app's authentication by intercepting the network traffic.
stretch_goals: Write the code patches to permanently fix the vulnerabilities you discovered.

title: CI/CD & Final Deployment
description: Set up automated pipelines (GitHub Actions) to run your JUnit/Python tests automatically when code is pushed, and configure continuous deployment to your cloud servers.
deliverable: An enterprise-grade, automated deployment pipeline.
hints: Automation is key to modern development. If you push a fix to main, the server should update itself.
stretch_goals: Write a GitHub Action that automatically formats your Python code using `Black` before committing.

title: Documentation & README Polish
description: A project without documentation doesn't exist to recruiters. Write comprehensive, beautiful READMEs for every single repository involved in the Capstone.
deliverable: Professional markdown documentation with screenshots, architecture diagrams, and setup instructions.
hints: Assume the person reading your documentation has never seen your code. Walk them through how to run it step-by-step.
stretch_goals: Use a tool like Swagger or Postman to generate beautiful documentation for your API endpoints.

title: The Final Launch
description: Final review. Click every button, test every flow, check every checkbox on your 130-week GitHub Pages tracker, and formally publish your portfolio to the world.
deliverable: The culmination of 130 weeks of relentless discipline.
hints: Take a deep breath and celebrate. You've earned it.
stretch_goals: Write an article on Medium or Dev.to summarizing your 2.5-year journey and the biggest lessons you learned.
